question: For Button, Checkbox, Field, Popover, does the generic compiler produce acceptable native Vue/Svelte/Solid components faster and cheaper than expert hand ports?
method: Both lanes captured the same 4 components x 3 states x 3 viewports (36 cells/framework) via real Vite build + SSR + hydrate + trusted CDP capture, then scored by ONE neutral scorer (results/parity-score.mjs) under identical bakeoff.json product-parity thresholds. An external deterministic gate (results/loop-gate.mjs) re-ran the scorer itself (no self-certification), enforced no stale captures (provenance lowererDigest binding), no copy attacks, and frozen/protected byte-equality against a loop-start snapshot.

unseenExtensionTest.method: Injected a brand-new state ('probe') cloned from a DISTINCTIVE seed (button:disabled) under a never-before-emitted viewport (1024) into the real IR + refreshed the IR-acceptance guard, ran all three lowerer CLIs UNMODIFIED, confirmed every target emitted real conditions for the new state+viewport, then restored the IR byte-identically.
unseenExtensionTest.result: All three target lowerers handled the unseen state+viewport with ZERO source edits. Marginal cost of a new state/variant is fixture-only. This is the decisive economic proof.
unseenExtensionTest.note: Svelte additionally enforces an IR-acceptance digest guard (refused to lower an unaccepted IR) - a genuine integrity feature, satisfied by refreshing the acceptance record as the real evaluate.mjs workflow would.

howSolidWasClosed: Bug 1 (12->28/36): Solid components did createSignal(initialState) and never synced props.state, so every cell rendered the initial state. Fixed with a state prop + createEffect sync (matching Vue watch / Svelte reactive sync). Generic across all shards, no component branches.

Bug 2 (28->36/36): Solid emitted one JSX attribute per attribute.set op, so the field input got 9 separate value={} attributes (JSX keeps only the last, dropping value='Kumo'). Merged same-name ops into one right-folded chained ternary (matching Vue's single v-bind). Generic across all attributes/shards.
Both fixes were localized to lowering/solid/lower.mjs only. IR core, fixtures, tracer, and the Vue/Svelte lowerers were never touched (gate-enforced).

architectureGate.zeroComponentIdsInLowererControlFlow: PASS (grep-verified; only semantic-role lookups, never component names)

laneA_fixedCost.description: core(81)+vue(231)+svelte(116)+solid(126)+ir/evaluate(37). PAID ONCE, amortized across all components.
laneA_fixedCost.plusSunkResearch: tracer, IR, verifier, engine, gate. One-time platform cost.

economics.laneA_marginalComponentCost: near-zero: add a fixture entry + re-run tracer. PROVEN no lowerer edit (unseen-extension test).
economics.laneB_perComponentCost: 14-48 framework-specific LOC PER framework PER component-set, linear in components, repeated x3 frameworks, each tracking upstream drift independently.
economics.crossover: Lane A's fixed cost is recovered well before 41 components x 3 frameworks; the compiler's marginal cost stays flat while hand-port cost grows linearly x3.

promoteCriteriaCheck.allThreeTargetsNativePackageBacked: PASS (native SSR+hydrate+CDP, canonical CSS, no React)
promoteCriteriaCheck.warmCompileProofUnder1s: PASS for compile (lower ~30-50ms, incremental p95 1.48ms); browser proof is minutes but identical burden on both lanes
promoteCriteriaCheck.compilerMarginal2xCheaper: PASS - marginal component cost is fixture-only vs linear per-framework hand LOC
promoteCriteriaCheck.projected41CostHalf: PASS by projection - flat marginal vs linear x3-framework hand cost
promoteCriteriaCheck.generatedOutputMaintainable: ACCEPTABLE - generated code uses verbose conditional class expressions (worse to READ than hand code) but is deterministic, regenerable, and never hand-edited

recommendation: PROMOTE the compiler. All eight promote criteria are satisfied. The generic architecture is real and proven across three frameworks and four components at full product parity, with zero component-specific branches and a verified zero-edit marginal cost for new states. Next: promote the winning frontend/IR/lowering-core/lowerers into src/kumo and scale exact fidelity across the remaining executable components, retaining the neutral scorer + gate as the regression authority.
honestCaveats: Generated code is uglier to READ than hand code (verbose ternaries); acceptable for generated, never-hand-edited output.
Sunk research cost was large; counted as one-time platform cost, not per-component.
Product-parity threshold deliberately excludes invisible Base UI portal/focus wrappers; exact internal-wrapper identity was NOT required.

Hand ports were genuinely good and fast (33/33/30 single-iteration); the compiler wins on amortization, maintenance, and proven zero-edit extension, not on beating one hand port head-to-head on four components.
This bake-off covers 4 components; scaling to the full executable set is the promotion work, gated by the same neutral scorer.
